message.content:
You are working in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service (NestJS/TypeScript monorepo).

## Your Task
Fix ESLint `custom-rules/require-object-params` violations for Jira ticket RECO-660.
Ticket URL: https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-660

## Context
- Current branch: `RECO-659-fix-date-helpers-util-eslint`
- You must create a NEW branch from this branch (NOT from main)
- Branch name format: `RECO-660-<kebab-summary>`
- Commit format: `RECO-660: <imperative description>`

## ESLint Rule
`custom-rules/require-object-params` — all exported functions must use a single object destructuring parameter instead of positional params.

**Pattern:**
```typescript
// ❌ Before
export function myFn(id: string, name: string): void {}
// ✅ After
export function myFn({ id, name }: { id: string; name: string }): void {}
```

## Steps

### 1. Find the target file
Run ESLint to find files with violations:
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 src/ --format=compact 2>&1 | grep "require-object-params" | head -30
```
These tickets (RECO-660, 686-694, 696) are all ESLint fix tickets for specific files. Pick the most appropriate file for RECO-660 (likely a util or service file that hasn't been fixed yet).

### 2. Create branch
```bash
git checkout -b RECO-660-<descriptive-name>
```
Branch from current branch `RECO-659-fix-date-helpers-util-eslint`.

### 3. Run ESLint on target file
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 <target-file> 2>&1
```

### 4. Fix violations
- Change positional params to `{ param }: { param: type }` destructuring
- Update ALL internal calls to changed functions
- Find ALL callers: `grep -r "functionName" src/ test/ --include="*.ts" -l`
- Update every call site
- Do NOT use `{ ...param }` spread shortcuts

### 5. Update tests
Find and update all test files that call changed functions.

### 6. Run tests
```bash
npm run test:unit -- --testPathPatterns="<pattern>" 2>&1
```

### 7. Run type-check
```bash
npm run type-check 2>&1
```

### 8. Verify ESLint
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 <changed-files> 2>&1
```

### 9. Commit (only relevant files — NOT .husky/pre-push)
```bash
git add <specific-files-only>
git commit -m "RECO-660: <description>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

### 10. Push
```bash
git push -u origin RECO-660-<branch-name>
```

### 11. Create DRAFT PR
Base = `RECO-659-fix-date-helpers-util-eslint` (NOT main).

```bash
gh pr create --draft --base RECO-659-fix-date-helpers-util-eslint --title "RECO-660: ..." --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
### Ticket 🐗

JIRA task: [RECO-660](https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-660)
Design: N/A

### Changes 🍍

<describe changes>

### PR Checks 🐊

- [x] I've self-reviewed my code.
- [x] I've added the necessary labels.
- [x] I've added tests for my code.
- [ ] I've updated the documentation.

### Description / Ticket Summary 🐙

<ticket summary>

### Screenshots / Short Videos 🐮

N/A
EOF
)"
```

## Critical Rules
- NEVER modify `.husky/pre-push`
- NEVER use relative imports (use `@utils/`, `@modules/`, etc.)
- NO comments in code
- All functions need explicit return types
- Do NOT use `{ ...spread }` as shortcut

## Return
Return:
1. The new branch name created
2. The PR URL
3. Summary of files changed and what was fixed

content.text: Now I understand all the changes needed. The three functions in `color-match.util.ts` need to use object destructuring. Let me also check if there are other tickets' files to avoid overlap. Since I need to pick an appropriate file for RECO-660, `color-match.util.ts` is a good choice as it's a focused utility file. Let me create the branch and make the fixes.

input.description: Create new branch from current branch

content.content: Switched to a new branch 'RECO-660-fix-color-match-util-eslint'

content.text:
Now let me fix the `color-match.util.ts` file. The three functions need object destructuring params:

1. `getColorRGBToIdHash(colors: IProductColor[])` → `getColorRGBToIdHash({ colors }: { colors: IProductColor[] })`
2. `convertRGBToString(rgb: RGBColor)` → `convertRGBToString({ rgb }: { rgb: RGBColor })`
3. `convertStringToRGB(str: string)` → `convertStringToRGB({ str }: { str: string })`

### Ticket 🐗

JIRA task: [RECO-660](https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-660)
Design: N/A

### Changes 🍍

- Refactored `getColorRGBToIdHash`, `convertRGBToString`, and `convertStringToRGB` in `src/utils/color-match.util.ts` to use single object destructuring parameter pattern
- Updated all call sites in `color-match-worker.service.ts` and `process-style.service.ts`
- Updated unit tests in `color-match.util.unit-spec.ts`
- Updated e2e mock in `process-style.service.e2e-spec.ts`

### PR Checks 🐊

- [x] I've self-reviewed my code.
- [x] I've added the necessary labels.
- [x] I've added tests for my code.
- [ ] I've updated the documentation.

### Description / Ticket Summary 🐙

Fixes `custom-rules/require-object-params` ESLint violations in the color-match utility file. All three exported functions now use a single object destructuring parameter instead of positional params, consistent with the project's coding standards.

### Screenshots / Short Videos 🐮

N/A
EOF

content.content: https://github.com/customink/recommendations-service/pull/487

content.text:
Everything is complete. Here's the summary:

**Branch created:** `RECO-660-fix-color-match-util-eslint`

**PR URL:** https://github.com/customink/recommendations-service/pull/487

**Files changed:**

1. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/utils/color-match.util.ts` - Fixed 3 violations: `getColorRGBToIdHash`, `convertRGBToString`, and `convertStringToRGB` were refactored from positional params to single object destructuring params.

2. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/modules/color-match-worker/color-match-worker.service.ts` - Updated 2 call sites: `getColorRGBToIdHash(palette)` → `getColorRGBToIdHash({ colors: palette })` and `convertStringToRGB(selectedColorRGB)` → `convertStringToRGB({ str: selectedColorRGB })`.

3. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/modules/rest/process-style/process-style.service.ts` - Updated 1 call site: `convertRGBToString(color.rbgs[0])` → `convertRGBToString({ rgb: color.rbgs[0] })`.

4. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/unit-tests/color-match.util.unit-spec.ts` - Updated 5 call sites in unit tests to use the new object destructuring signatures.

5. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/test/rest/process-style.service.e2e-spec.ts` - Updated the `convertRGBToString` jest mock to accept `{ rgb }` destructured param.
